title: Lesson Quiz: Presenting a Career Portfolio
description: Assessment of the concept that Organizing and delivering a presentation.

questions.prompt: What should a portfolio presentation include?

choices.text: An introduction, selected artifacts, and explanation of what they show
choices.feedback: Correct. A portfolio presentation should introduce the portfolio and explain selected evidence clearly.
A strong presentation should explain the purpose of the artifacts, not just name them.

choices.text: Artifacts shown in random order with no closing statement
choices.feedback: Organization and a clear closing help the audience understand the presentation.

choices.text: Every portfolio item without deciding which ones matter most
choices.feedback: A presentation should be selective and focused on the strongest evidence.

questions.prompt: Artifacts in a portfolio presentation should be explained instead of only displayed.

choices.feedback: Artifacts should be explained so the audience understands why they matter.
Correct. Explaining artifacts helps connect them to skills, growth, and readiness.

questions.prompt: Why is a logical order important in a portfolio presentation?

choices.text: Because it helps the audience follow the presentation more easily
choices.feedback: This is a good reason, but the correct answer in this set explains both order and understanding of purpose.

choices.text: Because it removes the need for transitions
choices.feedback: Transitions still help connect ideas clearly.

choices.text: Because random order makes ideas stronger
choices.feedback: Random order usually makes a presentation harder to follow.

choices.text: Because it helps the audience understand the sequence and purpose of the information
choices.feedback: Correct. Logical order helps the audience follow the presentation and understand why each part matters.

questions.prompt: A student plans to present a leadership artifact first, then a resume draft, then a closing statement about readiness. What does this show?

choices.feedback: The order described shows structure, not the absence of it.

choices.text: The student is organizing the presentation in a purposeful way
choices.feedback: This is close, but the correct answer in this set more fully explains the role of order and evidence.

choices.text: The student is using a clear presentation plan that connects evidence to readiness
choices.feedback: Correct. The student has chosen an order that helps explain the evidence and its purpose.

choices.text: The student should remove the closing statement
choices.feedback: A closing statement can help summarize the main message of the presentation.

questions.prompt: A portfolio presentation should connect artifacts to strengths, growth, or readiness.

choices.feedback: Correct. Artifacts are stronger when the presenter explains what they show.
Portfolio presentations should clearly connect evidence to strengths, growth, or readiness.
